{{Quote|If you run, we shoot you down!|Grappa|<ref name="VP" />}}
'''Grappa''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] hired by the government of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Osarian]] to serve as a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Osarian First-Force]]. His hiring was a response by Osarian to the quickly escalating [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict]]; the planet's own military was too small and technologically limited to pose a threat to the neighboring world of [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]].

In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Grappa attempted to halt the ''[[Jade Sabre]]'', a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] carrying the former [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], who was on a diplomatic mission to Rhommamool. The head of the Osarian government, [[Shunta Osarian Dharrg]], wanted to forcibly arrange a meeting with Organa Solo in order to hinder her from inadvertently heightening the [[Nom Anor|Rhommamoolian leader's]] stature in the sector. Although Grappa's small [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of outdated [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] was initially able to outmaneuver the Republic ship, their attempts at redirecting the ''Jade Sabre'' were ultimately unsuccessful. Three of the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] under Grappa's command were incapacitated in the ensuing skirmish, forcing the remainder of the First-Force squadron to retreat.

==Biography==
===Service in the Osarian First-Force===
Some time prior to [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] Grappa was hired by the government of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Osarian]] to serve in the [[Osarian First-Force]], the [[Navy/Legends|spaceborne branch]] of the world's military forces. The authorities on Osarian were concerned that the [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict|escalating conflict]] with the neighboring planet, [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]], would erupt into war, and were thus looking to hire [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to complement their technologically inferior military.<ref name="VP" />

Grappa and his fellow mercenaries were supplied with [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] by the Osarian government. Although the [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were considered outdated by 25 ABY, the mercenaries were able to overcome this hindrance by employing advanced starfighter tactics. At some point, Grappa was granted the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the First-Force.<ref name="VP" />

===Encounter with the ''Jade Sabre''===
{{Quote|You come with us. To Osa-Prime.|Grappa|<ref name="VP" />}}
[[File:Jadesabre.jpg|thumb|The ''Jade Sabre'']]
In 25 ABY, Captain Grappa, still in Osarian employ, attempted to intercept a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], the ''[[Jade Sabre]]'', carrying former [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], who was on a diplomatic mission to Rhommamool. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] accompanied Organa Solo in the shuttle. Although Organa Solo's mission was to negotiate peace between the two planets, [[Shunta Osarian Dharrg]], head of the Osarian government, was worried that her visit would inadvertently cause the stature of [[Nom Anor]], the leader of the extremist [[Red Knights of Life]] organization that controlled Rhommamool, to increase, both on Rhommamool itself and in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] at large. Dharrg wished to meet with the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] before she could do so with Anor.<ref name="VP" />

Grappa approached the ''Jade Sabre'' with four Z-95 Headhunters—all piloted by fellow Rodians—hiding two more some distance away in case the ''Sabre'' did not come willingly. He made viewscreen contact with the shuttle, and demanded the passengers follow him and his [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] to [[Osa-Prime]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of Osarian, to meet with Shunta Osarian Dharrg. The crew aboard the shuttle refused, and attempted to flee the First-Force Headhunters by out-navigating them with their technologically superior shuttle, fearing that a gunfight would lead to a diplomatic crisis. Grappa and his fellow mercenaries were forced to open fire and bring forth the two covert fighters in order to keep up with the ''Jade Sabre''.<ref name="VP" />

Despite the fact that the shuttle was piloted by the Jedi Jaina Solo, whose [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitivity to the Force]] gave her an advantage, the First-Force Headhunters managed to position themselves in increasingly favorable shooting positions while pursuing the shuttle. Although the battle appeared to go in Grappa's favor at first, this changed when the ''Jade Sabre'' suddenly incapacitated one of his Headhunters by crashing into it. Within moments, a [[T-65XJ X-wing starfighter]] piloted by yet another Jedi, [[Wurth Skidder]], joined the battle, destroying two more Headhunters. With additional New Republic reinforcements approaching, in the form of a [[Mediator-class battle cruiser|''Mediator''-class battle cruiser]], the remaining First-Force pilots chose to retreat and abort their mission.<ref name="VP" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|They're good.|Mara Jade Skywalker, referring to Grappa and his squadron|<ref name="VP" />}}
Grappa was willing to risk his life by engaging high-profile New Republic officials in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]]. His adeptness at starfighter tactics was such that he and the ships under his command were able to successfully out-navigate the Force-sensitive Jedi pilot Jaina Solo, as well as causing Mara Jade Skywalker to consider his abilities "good." Grappa had the appearance of an average Rodian; his skin was [[Color/Legends|green]], his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were black, his head sported a spiny head ridge, and a tapir-like snout adorned his face. His voice was described as uneven, and he was able to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], albeit with a limited vocabulary.<ref name="VP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Grappa was created by the author [[R. A. Salvatore]]. His only appearance is in the novel [[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime|''Vector Prime'']] ([[1999]]) of the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''New Jedi Order'']] series.
